prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_retake_case student_individual_1)
(create_retake_case student_cohort_1)
(assign_retake_option_to_case student_individual_1 retake_option_a)
(assign_retake_option_to_case student_cohort_1 retake_option_b)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(validate_retake_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case student_cohort_1 assessment_event_1)
(validate_retake_case student_cohort_1 assessment_event_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case student_cohort_1 assessment_event_1)
(assign_staff_to_case student_individual_1 staff_member_1)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(reserve_component_slot_for_student student_individual_1 component_slot_1 assessment_event_1)
(confirm_student_preparation_with_staff student_individual_1 component_slot_1 staff_member_1)
(release_staff_from_case student_individual_1 staff_member_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(assign_staff_to_case student_cohort_1 staff_member_1)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case student_cohort_1 assessment_event_1)
(reserve_assessment_slot_for_cohort student_cohort_1 assessment_slot_1 assessment_event_1)
(confirm_cohort_preparation_with_staff student_cohort_1 assessment_slot_1 staff_member_1)
(release_staff_from_case student_cohort_1 staff_member_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case student_cohort_1 assessment_event_1)
(assemble_retake_offer_variant_1 student_individual_1 student_cohort_1 component_slot_1 assessment_slot_1 retake_offer_1)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(confirm_retake_offer_materials retake_offer_1 student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(apply_student_record_update_from_offer student_individual_1 retake_offer_1)
(apply_cohort_record_update_from_offer student_cohort_1 retake_offer_1)
(create_retake_case course_component_1)
(assign_retake_option_to_case course_component_1 retake_option_c)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case course_component_1 assessment_event_1)
(validate_retake_case course_component_1 assessment_event_1)
(bind_material_to_component_and_offer course_component_1 assessment_material_1 retake_offer_1)
(prepare_component_for_examiner_assignment course_component_1 assessment_material_1 retake_offer_1 assessment_event_1)
(assign_staff_to_case course_component_1 staff_member_1)
(assign_examiner_to_component course_component_1 examiner_assignment_1)
(assign_examiner_and_mark_component_variant_1 course_component_1 examiner_assignment_1 staff_member_1 assessment_material_1 retake_offer_1)
(assign_external_reviewer_to_component course_component_1 external_reviewer_1)
(record_external_reviewer_assignment_variant_1 course_component_1 external_reviewer_1 assessment_material_1 retake_offer_1)
(provisionally_complete_component course_component_1)
(record_provisional_completion_for_case_subject course_component_1 evidence_document_1 assessment_event_1)
(finalize_component_record_and_release_option course_component_1 retake_option_c evidence_document_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case course_component_1 assessment_event_1)
(release_staff_from_case course_component_1 staff_member_1)
(unassign_examiner_from_component course_component_1 examiner_assignment_1)
(unassign_external_reviewer_from_component course_component_1 external_reviewer_1)
(create_retake_case course_component_2)
(assign_retake_option_to_case course_component_2 retake_option_c)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case course_component_2 assessment_event_1)
(validate_retake_case course_component_2 assessment_event_1)
(bind_material_to_component_and_offer course_component_2 assessment_material_2 retake_offer_1)
(prepare_component_for_examiner_assignment course_component_2 assessment_material_2 retake_offer_1 assessment_event_1)
(assign_staff_to_case course_component_2 staff_member_1)
(assign_examiner_to_component course_component_2 examiner_assignment_1)
(assign_examiner_and_mark_component_variant_1 course_component_2 examiner_assignment_1 staff_member_1 assessment_material_2 retake_offer_1)
(assign_external_reviewer_to_component course_component_2 external_reviewer_1)
(record_external_reviewer_assignment_variant_1 course_component_2 external_reviewer_1 assessment_material_2 retake_offer_1)
(provisionally_complete_component course_component_2)
(record_provisional_completion_for_case_subject course_component_2 evidence_document_1 assessment_event_1)
(finalize_component_record_and_release_option course_component_2 retake_option_c evidence_document_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case course_component_2 assessment_event_1)
(release_staff_from_case course_component_2 staff_member_1)
(unassign_examiner_from_component course_component_2 examiner_assignment_1)
(unassign_external_reviewer_from_component course_component_2 external_reviewer_1)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(record_provisional_completion_for_case_subject student_individual_1 evidence_document_1 assessment_event_1)
(finalize_student_record_and_release_option student_individual_1 retake_option_a evidence_document_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case student_individual_1 assessment_event_1)
(reserve_assessment_event_for_case student_cohort_1 assessment_event_1)
(record_provisional_completion_for_case_subject student_cohort_1 evidence_document_1 assessment_event_1)
(finalize_cohort_member_record_and_release_option student_cohort_1 retake_option_b evidence_document_1)
(release_assessment_event_from_case student_cohort_1 assessment_event_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a planning domain for retaking sequences for failed components. It's complex with many predicates and actions. The goal is to update the academic records for student_individual_1, student_cohort_1, course_component_1, and course_component_2.


## Investigating Planning Domain  
- The problem focuses on a planning domain for retaking failed course components.  
- It involves complex predicates and actions to manage academic updates.  

## Updating Academic Records  
- The goal is to update records for student_individual_1, student_cohort_1, course_component_1, and course_component_2.  
- This process ensures accurate tracking of retakes and academic progress.

## Exploring Retake Processes  
- Course components are treated as retake subjects, alongside individual and cohort students, requiring similar update processes.  
- Actions like finalize_student_record_and_release_option and finalize_component_record_and_update_record are needed to achieve academic_record_updated for all subjects.  

## Checking Prerequisites  
- Updating records requires final_grade_recorded, assigned_retake_option, linked evidence, and not updated as preconditions.  
- final_grade_recorded depends on retake_subject_grading_checkpoint_passed, which involves preparation confirmed, readiness for retake, and material assignment.  

## Outlining a Plan  
- Start by creating retake cases for student_individual_1, student_cohort_1, course_component_1, and course_component_2, as they aren’t registered or updated yet.  
- Sequence involves creating cases, assigning retake options, reserving assessment events, and validating, with only one assessment_event_1 available.

## Outlining Preparation Steps  
- Preparing students for retakes involves confirming preparation with staff or allocating support resources.  
- Student readiness (student_ready_for_retake) is set by confirming preparation or finalizing student preparation, with similar steps for cohorts.

## Assembling Retake Offers  
- Retake offers have four variants, requiring preparation confirmed, readiness, and reserved slots for both individuals and cohorts.  
- Variant 1, for example, needs preparation confirmed, slots reserved, and readiness for both, alongside an available offer.

## Sequencing Individual Preparation  
- For student_individual_1, start with creating a retake case, assign a retake option (e.g., retake_option_a), and reserve assessment_event_1, then validate.  
- After validation, assign staff, reserve component slots, and confirm preparation, marking the student as ready.

## Managing Assessment Events  
- Assessment_event_1 becomes unavailable after reservation, limiting use for others unless released.  
- Events can be released after validation (release_assessment_event_from_case), making them available again while maintaining validated status, allowing reuse for other cases like student_cohort_1.

## Managing Retake Options  
- Finalizing records requires academic_record_updated, achievable only through finalize_record_and_release_option, necessitating assigned_retake_options.  
- With only three options for four subjects, plan to complete one subject fully, release the option, then reuse for the next.  

## Prioritizing Subject Preparation  
- Academic_record_updated applies to students and components, but finalize_exception_and_update_record sets component-specific updates, not general ones.  
- For components, provisionally_complete_component requires component_grading_checkpoint_passed, tied to offers and external reviewer assignments.  

## Exploring Exception Paths  
- Assigning external reviewers to exceptions requires prior approval and component validation, setting external_review_complete upon completion.  
- Finalizing exceptions updates component_academic_record and grading checkpoints, but doesn’t directly achieve academic_record_updated for subjects.

## Linking Student and Cohort Preparation  
- Creating a retake offer requires both individual students and cohort students to be prepared, as offer assembly ties component slots and assessment slots together.  
- Individual student preparation can’t proceed without cohort readiness, indicating a coupled process for offer creation.  

## Sequencing Subject Completion  
- With only three retake options for four subjects, one option must be reused by completing a subject fully before reassigning the option.  
- Components might use exception paths like finalize_exception_and_update_record for grading checkpoints, but still require assigned options for final records.  

## Planning Case Registration  
- Start by registering retake cases for all four subjects: student_individual_1, student_cohort_1, course_component_1, and course_component_2.  
- Assigning options initially may prioritize students and one component, but all subjects ultimately need options to finalize records.  

## Exploring Alternative Paths  
- Components may attempt paths without offers, like exceptions, but still need finalize_component_record for academic_record_updated, requiring options.  
- No way exists to set academic_record_updated without finalize_record actions, confirming all subjects must follow the option assignment path.

## Assembling Retake Offers  
- Retake offer creation requires both individual and cohort students to be prepared, with effects including offer creation and linked component/assessment slots.  
- After assembling, the offer is no longer available, setting the stage for material assignment and further student updates.  

## Managing Student Scheduling  
- Confirming retake offer materials is specific to individual students, requiring the offer to be created and the student scheduled for an assessment event.  
- After cohort preparation, the event must be reserved for the individual student again to meet scheduling requirements for material confirmation.  

## Updating Student Records  
- Applying student and cohort record updates from the offer requires materials to be assigned, setting retake_subject_grading_checkpoint_passed for both.  
- Both processes rely on prior preparation, readiness, and offer creation, ensuring checkpoints are passed via shared offer materials.  

## Planning Component Validation  
- Components haven’t had cases registered yet, with two components needing one of the three available options for retake processing.  
- The next step involves validating components, likely prioritizing one component with the remaining option to proceed with grading.

## Managing Component Scheduling  
- Course component 1 needs to be scheduled for an assessment event after reserving it, ensuring materials can be bound later.  
- Scheduling is required before preparing the component for examiner assignment, aligning with the variant 1 process.  

## Binding Component Materials  
- Binding materials to course component 1 requires validation, linked offer, and availability, all confirmed after offer creation.  
- Once bound, materials enable further steps like preparing for examiner assignment, ensuring readiness for grading.  

## Assigning Examiner and Staff  
- Assigning an examiner to course component 1 requires validation and examiner availability, setting the stage for marking.  
- Staff must also be assigned to the component, ensuring all roles are filled for the grading process to proceed.  

## Preparing for External Review  
- After examiner assignment, an external reviewer can be assigned to course component 1, requiring validation and reviewer availability.  
- This step ensures additional oversight, completing the setup for recording the external review in variant 1.

## Reviewing Student Preparation  
- Student and cohort cases were created for retake options, with individual student 1 assigned option A and cohort 1 assigned option B.  
- Validation required reserving and releasing assessment events for both individual and cohort cases to ensure availability.  

## Managing Staff and Slots  
- Staff were assigned and assessment events reserved for both individual and cohort preparations, ensuring slots were confirmed with staff.  
- After preparation, staff and events were released to maintain availability for subsequent steps, like assembling the offer.  

## Ensuring Sequence Order  
- The process confirmed materials assignment requires scheduled individual events, but applying record updates does not.  
- Assembling the offer and cohort preparation can proceed without scheduling, maintaining flexibility in the sequence.  

## Confirming Policy and Support  
- Policy documents weren’t used, and no exceptions were requested, aligning with the confirm-with-staff path.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
